text: When designing a REST API, it is important to follow best practices to ensure it is easy to use and maintain. Endpoints should be clear and logical, such as `/users` for user data rather than `/getUsers`. Use standard HTTP status codes to communicate results: `200 OK` for success, `201 Created` for new resources, `400 Bad Request` for client errors, and `404 Not Found` when a resource does not exist. Always include versioning in your API, for example `/v1/users`, so changes do not break existing clients. Finally, provide thorough documentation that explains each endpoint, its parameters, and expected responses. Good documentation helps developers integrate with your API quickly and correctly.
